title: Add Deep14x416 KV2 non-record MLX submission (val_bpb=1.8440)
body:
This PR adds a non-record unlimited-compute submission under `records/track_non_record_16mb/`.

The user-facing effect is a new reproducible Apple Silicon MLX result in the repository: a deeper/narrower SP-1024 model with 14 layers at width 416 and 2 KV heads, trained locally on an Apple M5 Max for 750 steps against a 10-shard FineWeb subset. The final post-quantized roundtrip metric recorded in the included log is `val_bpb=1.84404368`, with an int8+zlib model payload of `12,339,367` bytes and total submission size of `12,388,989` bytes.

The underlying motivation was to explore a simple parameter-budget trade: reduce width slightly, add depth, and use more aggressive KV sharing while staying well under the 16 MB artifact limit. This submission keeps the trainer straightforward by reusing the repository `train_gpt_mlx.py` snapshot exactly, and only changes the runtime configuration through environment variables. To make full validation tractable on local Apple Silicon hardware, the run also uses a larger validation batch and logit chunking; these settings affect execution efficiency, not the metric definition itself.

The root cause this PR addresses is not a bug in the repo but a gap in the records folder: there was no local Apple Silicon submission documenting this deeper/narrower 14x416 KV2 configuration and its measured result. The fix is therefore additive only: a new record folder containing the copied training script, the exact train log, a README with command/config details, and `submission.json` metadata.

Validation for this PR was done by actually running the training job to completion locally, then checking the copied script compiles with `python -m py_compile`. The included `train.log` contains the full training trace, the pre-quant validation result, the compressed model size, and the final `final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact` metric.

comments.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Invoke the archived trainer, not the mutable repo-root script**

As written, `python3 train_gpt_mlx.py` only works if the user runs this README from the repo root, but in that case it executes the top-level `train_gpt_mlx.py` instead of the snapshot archived in this record. Running the command inside the record folder does pick up the archived script, but that script defaults `DATA_PATH` and `TOKENIZER_PATH` to `./data/...` (`train_gpt_mlx.py` lines 45-46), and `load_validation_tokens()` raises when that local `./data` tree is missing (`train_gpt_mlx.py` lines 725-728). So there is currently no invocation here that both uses the preserved script and reproduces the logged run.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Fixed in f9ce10b. The README command now explicitly runs from the archived record folder, calls `./train_gpt_mlx.py`, and sets `DATA_PATH` / `TOKENIZER_PATH` relative to that folder so the preserved snapshot and published dataset/tokenizer are used together reproducibly.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Add the required `train_gpt.py` entrypoint**

The repository’s submission contract still treats `train_gpt.py` as the artifact entrypoint: `README.md:133` says all counted code must live in that file, and `README.md:167` says every record folder must include a `train_gpt.py` that compiles and runs in place. This record only ships `train_gpt_mlx.py`, so any reviewer or automation following the documented submission flow can reject the run or measure `bytes_code` against the wrong file. Please add a `train_gpt.py` wrapper/copy (or update the submission contract in the same change) before landing this record.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
Fixed in 9159214. The record now ships the archived trainer under `train_gpt.py`, the README invokes `python3 ./train_gpt.py`, and the entrypoint still compiles cleanly with `python -m py_compile`.

**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Pin the exact FineWeb train shards for this run**

Pointing `DATA_PATH` at the generic `fineweb10B_sp1024` directory is not enough to reproduce this result. The archived trainer consumes every `fineweb_train_*.bin` under that path (`train_gpt.py`, `TokenStream.__init__`), while the attached log only says that `10/195` shards happened to be present. If someone reruns this from a normal published export, or from a different 10-shard subset, they will train on different data and get a different score. Please record the exact shard filenames (or include a subset manifest) in the record entry.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Addressed the remaining reproducibility gap in e13e8db. The record now includes `train_shards.txt` with the exact 10 FineWeb train shards used for the run, and the README stages a local `data_subset` containing only those train shards plus the fixed validation shard before invoking `train_gpt.py`.
